title: Yogi Bear's Supersonic Space Adventure

He cracked the code on how heat moves through objects, creating a whole new field of study. But Fourier wasn't just about numbers - he also loved exploring ancient Egypt's mysteries!
activity_question: What did Joseph Fourier create a theory about?
follow_up_question: Besides math, what other subject fascinated Fourier?

title: A Founding Father's Welsh Roots
story: Francis Lewis wasn't born in America, but he helped create it! This future signer of the Declaration of Independence came into the world in Llandaff, Wales in 1713. On March 21 of that year, baby Francis began a journey that would lead him across the ocean. He grew up to become a merchant and one of America's Founding Fathers.
activity_question: Where was Francis Lewis born?
follow_up_question: What important document did Francis Lewis sign?

title: Olympic Star Sprints into History
story: Zoom! Walter Tewksbury raced into the world on March 21, 1876. This speedy American would later become a track and field superstar. At the 1920 Olympics, Tewksbury dashed his way to an amazing collection of medals: two gold, two silver, and one bronze! But that's not all - when he wasn't running, he was fixing teeth as a dentist.
activity_question: What profession did Walter Tewksbury have besides being an athlete?
follow_up_question: How many total Olympic medals did Walter Tewksbury win?
